seo.title: Cuba Market Intelligence | Perfect Mission
seo.description: Cuba has enormous theoretical upside, but it remains a watchlist market rather than an immediately executable one.

hero.title: Cuba: huge theoretical upside, not yet a current platform market
hero.subtitle: The thesis is obvious. The timing still is not.

summary.one_line_verdict: Cuba is still a watchlist market: strategically fascinating, operationally premature.

market_stage.label: Not yet executable
market_stage.description: Recent reform signals are real, but they sit inside an ongoing energy, currency, and sanctions crisis that still makes immediate-scale execution unattractive.

summary.operator_angle: The market could become exceptional later. Right now it is a timing bet, not an operating base.
summary.investor_angle: Every sophisticated investor understands the upside conceptually; the problem is that the implementation window is still unclear and politically conditioned.
summary.base_case: Keep Cuba visible on the site as a strategic watchlist market, not as an immediate deployment geography.

quick_facts.label: Reform signal
quick_facts.value: Late-2025 proposals included allowing foreign companies to purchase real estate, hire directly, and operate more flexibly

quick_facts.label: Official measure signal
quick_facts.value: Cuban authorities formally announced measures to streamline and boost foreign investment in November 2025

quick_facts.label: Investment-approval signal
quick_facts.value: Official reporting says 32 new businesses were approved in 2025 with committed capital of US$2.1 billion

quick_facts.label: Crisis signal
quick_facts.value: Independent official reporting describes the current crisis as the most severe in decades

strategy_fit.why: The timing and operating environment are not yet good enough.
Cuba deserves continuous monitoring because the upside could be enormous if the operating regime truly opens.
Only for parties with deep patience and political tolerance.

development_reality.headline: Watch closely, but do not confuse signals with readiness
development_reality.copy: Cuba matters because it may eventually be the biggest rerating story in the wider map. The recent signals are meaningful, but the country remains constrained by crisis conditions, state control, and geopolitical risk.
avoid_first: assuming the market is open because reforms were announced
treating late-2025 measures as equivalent to a stable investable regime
process_notes: The immediate execution thesis remains weak.
Cuba should stay on the map, but in a separate watchlist category.

foreign_ownership.summary: The formal regime may be loosening, but direct and reliable foreign property participation is still not simple enough to market as current execution.
foreign_ownership.caution: Treat all ‘opening’ narratives as provisional until regulations, enforceability, and operations visibly change.

priority_cities.thesis: The place everyone watches first, but still not a clean current-operating market.
Relevant for hospitality logic later, not a default current deployment zone.
Important for monitoring foreign-investment regime changes.

catalysts: Policy language around foreign investment is becoming more flexible.
Real estate has been explicitly mentioned in the reform discussion.
If the regime genuinely opens, Cuba could become one of the largest rerating stories in the hemisphere.
risks: Severe ongoing energy and economic crisis.
The risk of being early is much higher than the risk of missing the very first move.

lifestyle_base.copy: Cuba is not a rational current operating base for the platform.
lifestyle_base.connectivity: Present but constrained by wider operating issues.

cta.headline: Need the verified version?
cta.body: Request a country memo or submit a specific site, listing, plot, or market thesis for verification.
